images.caption: Figure 1:Checkpoint deltas show thatOPD-style updates are small, coordinate-sparse, spectrally concentrated, and off-principal. Gray bars are reference runs rather thanOPDruns: Qwen-Math Distill is an offline distillation-style contrast, and the remaining gray bars areRLVRreferences.

Figure 2:Layerwise and per-parameter-matrix update sparsity for DS-Qwen OPD. Sparsity counts coordinates whose delta between stored checkpoint weights is numerically indistinguishable from zero at tolerance10−510^{-5}.

Figure 3:Comparison ofOPDreasoning performance along the training process, reported as average validation accuracy over AIME24 and AIME25. Left: fullOPDversusOPDtrained with theOPD,RLVR, or random masks. Right: AdamW versus SGD in the JustRL-teacherOPDsetting. Benchmark-wise curves are shown in AppendixA.7.

Figure 4:Benchmark-wise breakdown of the subnetwork-maskedOPDexperiment in Figure3(a). Left: AIME24. Right: AIME25. TheOPDmask tracks the full run closely on both benchmarks, while the random mask remains weaker.

Figure 5:Benchmark-wise breakdown of the AdamW-versus-SGD experiment in Figure3(b). Left: AIME24. Right: AIME25. In the JustRL-teacherOPDsetting, SGD lags behind AdamW on both benchmarks at the final evaluation point.

Figure 6:AdamW optimizer-state diagnostics for JustRL-teacherOPD. Left: cosine similarity between AdamW momentum and the current gradient. Right: coefficient of variation ofvt\sqrt{v_{t}}, computed asstd​(vt)/mean​(vt)\mathrm{std}(\sqrt{v_{t}})/\mathrm{mean}(\sqrt{v_{t}}). Momentum alignment is initially high but unstable, while the second-moment CV remains large throughout training.
Figure 7:Qwen2.5-VL subnetwork-maskedOPDon Geo3K. The full-OPDmask closely tracks the full run, while density-matched random masks and the smallerGRPOmask underperform.
